english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Scimus enim quoniam si terrestris domus nostra hujus habitationis dissolvatur, quod ædificationem ex Deo habemus, domum non manufactam, æternam in cælis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδαμεν γὰρ ὅτι ἐὰν ἡ ἐπίγειος ἡμῶν οἰκία τοῦ σκήνους καταλυθῇ οἰκοδομὴν ἐκ θεοῦ ἔχομεν οἰκίαν ἀχειροποίητον αἰώνιον ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς
verses.king_james: For we know that if our earthly house of *this* tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam et in hoc ingemiscimus, habitationem nostram, quæ de cælo est, superindui cupientes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ ἐν τούτῳ στενάζομεν τὸ οἰκητήριον ἡμῶν τὸ ἐξ οὐρανοῦ ἐπενδύσασθαι ἐπιποθοῦντες
verses.king_james: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:

verses.latin_vulgate: si tamen vestiti, non nudi inveniamur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴγε καὶ ἐνδυσάμενοι οὐ γυμνοὶ εὑρεθησόμεθα
verses.king_james: If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam et qui sumus in hoc tabernaculo, ingemiscimus gravati: eo quod nolumus expoliari, sed supervestiri, ut absorbeatur quod mortale est, a vita.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ οἱ ὄντες ἐν τῷ σκήνει στενάζομεν βαρούμενοι ἐπειδή οὐ θέλομεν ἐκδύσασθαι ἀλλ᾽ ἐπενδύσασθαι ἵνα καταποθῇ τὸ θνητὸν ὑπὸ τῆς ζωῆς
verses.king_james: For we that are in *this* tabernacle do groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality might be swallowed up of life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem efficit nos in hoc ipsum, Deus, qui dedit nobis pignus Spiritus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ κατεργασάμενος ἡμᾶς εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο θεός ὁ καὶ δοὺς ἡμῖν τὸν ἀῤῥαβῶνα τοῦ πνεύματος
verses.king_james: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing *is* God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audentes igitur semper, scientes quoniam dum sumus in corpore, peregrinamur a Domino
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θαῤῥοῦντες οὖν πάντοτε καὶ εἰδότες ὅτι ἐνδημοῦντες ἐν τῷ σώματι ἐκδημοῦμεν ἀπὸ τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: Therefore *we are* always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:

verses.latin_vulgate: (per fidem enim ambulamus, et non per speciem):
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ πίστεως γὰρ περιπατοῦμεν οὐ διὰ εἴδους
verses.king_james: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)

verses.latin_vulgate: audemus autem, et bonam voluntatem habemus magis peregrinari a corpore, et præsentes esse ad Dominum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θαῤῥοῦμεν δὲ καὶ εὐδοκοῦμεν μᾶλλον ἐκδημῆσαι ἐκ τοῦ σώματος καὶ ἐνδημῆσαι πρὸς τὸν κύριον
verses.king_james: We are confident, *I say*, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ideo contendimus, sive absentes, sive præsentes, placere illi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ καὶ φιλοτιμούμεθα εἴτε ἐνδημοῦντες εἴτε ἐκδημοῦντες εὐάρεστοι αὐτῷ εἶναι
verses.king_james: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes enim non manifestari oportet ante tribunal Christi, ut referat unusquisque propria corporis, prout gessit, sive bonum, sive malum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοὺς γὰρ πάντας ἡμᾶς φανερωθῆναι δεῖ ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ βήματος τοῦ χριστοῦ ἵνα κομίσηται ἕκαστος τὰ διὰ τοῦ σώματος πρὸς ἃ ἔπραξεν εἴτε ἀγαθὸν εἴτε κακόν
verses.king_james: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things *done* in *his* body, according to that he hath done, whether *it be* good or bad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scientes ergo timorem Domini, hominibus suademus, Deo autem manifesti sumus. Spero autem et in conscientiis vestris manifestos nos esse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδότες οὖν τὸν φόβον τοῦ κυρίου ἀνθρώπους πείθομεν θεῷ δὲ πεφανερώμεθα ἐλπίζω δὲ καὶ ἐν ταῖς συνειδήσεσιν ὑμῶν πεφανερῶσθαι
verses.king_james: Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your consciences.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non iterum commendamus nos vobis, sed occasionem damus vobis gloriandi pro nobis: ut habeatis ad eos qui in facie gloriantur, et non in corde.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ πάλιν ἑαυτοὺς συνιστάνομεν ὑμῖν ἀλλὰ ἀφορμὴν διδόντες ὑμῖν καυχήματος ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν ἵνα ἔχητε πρὸς τοὺς ἐν προσώπῳ καυχωμένους καὶ οὐ καρδίᾳ
verses.king_james: For we commend not ourselves again unto you, but give you occasion to glory on our behalf, that ye may have somewhat to *answer* them which glory in appearance, and not in heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sive enim mente excedimus Deo: sive sobrii sumus, vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε γὰρ ἐξέστημεν θεῷ εἴτε σωφρονοῦμεν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: For whether we be beside ourselves, *it is* to God: or whether we be sober, *it is* for your cause.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caritas enim Christi urget nos: æstimantes hoc, quoniam si unus pro omnibus mortuus est, ergo omnes mortui sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ ἀγάπη τοῦ χριστοῦ συνέχει ἡμᾶς κρίναντας τοῦτο ὅτι εἰ εἷς ὑπὲρ πάντων ἀπέθανεν ἄρα οἱ πάντες ἀπέθανον
verses.king_james: For the love of Christ constraineth us; because we thus judge, that if one died for all, then were all dead:

verses.latin_vulgate: et pro omnibus mortuus est Christus: ut, et qui vivunt, jam non sibi vivant, sed ei qui pro ipsis mortuus est et resurrexit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑπὲρ πάντων ἀπέθανεν ἵνα οἱ ζῶντες μηκέτι ἑαυτοῖς ζῶσιν ἀλλὰ τῷ ὑπὲρ αὐτῶν ἀποθανόντι καὶ ἐγερθέντι
verses.king_james: And *that* he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto him which died for them, and rose again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque nos ex hoc neminem novimus secundum carnem. Et si cognovimus secundum carnem Christum, sed nunc jam non novimus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ἡμεῖς ἀπὸ τοῦ νῦν οὐδένα οἴδαμεν κατὰ σάρκα εἰ δὲ καὶ ἐγνώκαμεν κατὰ σάρκα χριστόν ἀλλὰ νῦν οὐκέτι γινώσκομεν
verses.king_james: Wherefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we *him* no more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si qua ergo in Christo nova creatura, vetera transierunt: ecce facta sunt omnia nova.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε εἴ τις ἐν χριστῷ καινὴ κτίσις τὰ ἀρχαῖα παρῆλθεν ἰδού, γέγονεν καινά τὰ πάντα
verses.king_james: Therefore if any man *be* in Christ, *he is* a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia autem ex Deo, qui nos reconciliavit sibi per Christum: et dedit nobis ministerium reconciliationis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ δὲ πάντα ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ καταλλάξαντος ἡμᾶς ἑαυτῷ διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ καὶ δόντος ἡμῖν τὴν διακονίαν τῆς καταλλαγῆς
verses.king_james: And all things *are* of God, who hath reconciled us to himself by Jesus Christ, and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation;

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam quidem Deus erat in Christo mundum reconcilians sibi, non reputans illis delicta ipsorum, et posuit in nobis verbum reconciliationis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς ὅτι θεὸς ἦν ἐν χριστῷ κόσμον καταλλάσσων ἑαυτῷ μὴ λογιζόμενος αὐτοῖς τὰ παραπτώματα αὐτῶν καὶ θέμενος ἐν ἡμῖν τὸν λόγον τῆς καταλλαγῆς
verses.king_james: To wit, that God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pro Christo ergo legatione fungimur, tamquam Deo exhortante per nos. Obsecramus pro Christo, reconciliamini Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπὲρ χριστοῦ οὖν πρεσβεύομεν ὡς τοῦ θεοῦ παρακαλοῦντος δι᾽ ἡμῶν δεόμεθα ὑπὲρ χριστοῦ καταλλάγητε τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: Now then we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God did beseech *you* by us: we pray *you* in Christ's stead, be ye reconciled to God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eum, qui non noverat peccatum, pro nobis peccatum fecit, ut nos efficeremur justitia Dei in ipso.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν γὰρ μὴ γνόντα ἁμαρτίαν ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν ἁμαρτίαν ἐποίησεν ἵνα ἡμεῖς γινώμεθα δικαιοσύνη θεοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: For he hath made him *to be* sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him.
